zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos於大約1185年出生在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利瓦爾蒙特時，他的父親Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont16歲，母親Mathilde d'Eu16歲。他和Agnes de Ponthieu至少有5個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1258年8月15日在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利艾斯陶迪維爾-艾卡利斯享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos于大约1185年出生在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利瓦爾蒙特时，他的父亲Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont16岁，母亲Mathilde d'Eu16岁。他和Agnes de Ponthieu至少有5个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1258年8月15日在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利艾斯陶迪維爾-艾卡利斯享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos was born about 1185, in Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, his father, Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont, was 16 and his mother, Mathilde d'Eu, was 16. He had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Agnes de Ponthieu. He died on 15 August 1258, in Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean I d’Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos est né environ 1185, à Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, son père, Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Mathilde d'Eu, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles avec Agnes de Ponthieu. Il est décédé le 15 août 1258, à Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos um 1185 in Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont 16 und seine Mutter Mathilde d'Eu 16. Er hatte mit Agnes de Ponthieu mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 15. August 1258 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos è nato il circa 1185 nel Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Mathilde d'Eu, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie con Agnes de Ponthieu. Morì il 15 agosto 1258, nel Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramosが約1185年にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメヴァルモントで生まれた時，父Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmontは16歳，母Mathilde d'Euは16歳であった。彼はAgnes de Ponthieuとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1258年8月15日にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメエストウテヴィレ-eカレスで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos(이)가 대략 1185년 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 발몽에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde d'Eu(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Agnes de Ponthieu(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1258년 8월 15일 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 에스토우테비레-e캘레스에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos nasceu aproximadamente 1185, em Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, seu pai, Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde d'Eu, tinha 16 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas com Agnes de Ponthieu. Ele faleceu em 15 de agosto de 1258, em Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos родился около 1185 г. в местности валмонт, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Mathilde d'Eu, 16. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери от Agnes de Ponthieu. Он умер 15 августа 1258 г. в местности эстоутевилле-eкаллесь, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean I de Estouteville, Sire d'Estouteville de Valmont et de Ramos nació aproximadamente 1185, en Valmont, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Henry I d'Estouteville - Lord of Elkington, Sgr d’Estouteville et Rames et de Valmont, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Mathilde d'Eu, tenía 16 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas con Agnes de Ponthieu. Murió el 15 de agosto de 1258, en Estouteville-Écalles, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 74 años.
